steps.title: What does an x-intercept represent?
steps.content:
An x-intercept is where the graph crosses the x-axis, where $y = 0$.

For this problem, that means $h(x) = 0$. Since $h(x)$ represents height in feet, the equation $h(x) = 0$ means the projectile is at ground level.

The x-coordinate of the x-intercept is the time (in seconds) when this occurs.

steps.title: Identify the positive x-intercept and its meaning
steps.content:
At $x = 0$, the height is $h(0) = 10$ feet — the projectile starts above ground.

Since the coefficient of $x^2$ is $-16$ (negative), the parabola opens downward.

Starting above ground with a downward-opening parabola, the projectile will eventually return to ground level at some positive time.

The positive x-intercept represents this time when the projectile hits the ground.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
